prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options: 10, 10, 10, 10, 9. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.
responses:
to determine the most desirable choice among the options 10 10 10 10 9 we apply the four intuitive properties and the prioritization rules provided

1  uniqueness an object is unique if it is the only one with a given property in the list 10 10 10 10 9 the value 9 is unique because it is the only object with that value while each object could be considered unique in its position eg the 3rd element is the only 3rd element the valuebased property is more fundamental in this context
2  extremeness an object is extreme if it has the largest or smallest feature the value 9 is the only object that is the minimum of the set the value 10 is the maximum but it is not unique in this property as there are four 10s thus 9 is the only object that is extreme in a unique way
3  centrality an object is central if it is the point around which the domain is symmetric in the list 10 10 10 10 9 the 3rd 10 is at the central position though the list values are not symmetric
4  uniqueness complement an object is a uniqueness complement if it is the only one without a given property the value 9 is the only object that does not have the property of being 10

prioritization
the instructions state to prioritize the selection of the only object that satisfies one of these properties 9 is the only object that is unique as the value 9 and the only object that is extreme as the minimum the 3rd 10 is the only object that is central in its position
according to the priority order unique then extreme then central and eventually unique complement 9 is chosen first as it satisfies the highestpriority properties of being unique and extreme
